senators.name: Steve Daines
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in chemical engineering from Montana State University. 13 years at Procter & Gamble managing operations domestically and in Asia. VP at RightNow Technologies (cloud computing company). Chemical engineering degree provides rigorous quantitative and scientific foundation applied in global industry.
education: B.S. Chemical Engineering, Montana State University

senators.name: Tim Sheehy
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. in history from U.S. Naval Academy. Navy SEAL officer with extensive combat deployments. Founded Bridger Aerospace (aerial firefighting). USNA education provides disciplined analytical training. Military special operations and business entrepreneurship show strategic capability but policy approach is more instinct-driven than evidence-based.
experience: U.S. Navy SEAL Officer and Team Leader
Combat deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan

MT-01.name: Ryan Zinke
MT-01.notNerdReason: B.S. from University of Oregon. Navy SEAL Commander (23 years). Former U.S. Secretary of the Interior. Military leadership at elite level requires analytical capability but governance record as Interior Secretary was marked by controversy rather than evidence-based management.

MT-02.name: Troy Downing
MT-02.nerdReason: Studied applied mathematics and computer science at New York University. Research scientist and educator at the Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at NYU — one of the world's premier mathematics research centers. Air Force veteran with combat search and rescue missions in Afghanistan. Founded technology companies. Combines genuine STEM research credentials with military analytical discipline.
education: Applied Mathematics and Computer Science, New York University (Courant Institute)
experience: U.S. Air Force, Combat Search and Rescue
